MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jul-28-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Not contested and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Coastside Lumber and Supply, Inc.'s Motion for Summary Judgment is off calendar. Defendant failed to provide the Court with courtesy copies as required under Local Rule 2.7B. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jul-28-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances, not contested and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.'s Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. Defendants failed to sustain its burden of showing that Plaintiffs do not possess, and cannot reasonably obtain, needed evidence that decedent was exposed to asbestos containing products attributable to Defendant. (Aguilar v. Atl. Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal. 4th 826, 855.) Defendant failed to address Plaintiffs' interrogatory responses identifying Defendant's Fire-Rated drywall as containing asbestos. (Defendant's Exhibit D [Plaintiff's Response to Defendant's Special 'Interrogatories, Set One] at 14:15-23.) Plaintiffs' objection to the declaration of Dr. Carl J. Rossi, Jr. is SUSTAINED in its entirety based on lack of foundation and lack of personal knowledge.

Defendants' motion for summary adjudication in the alternative is DENIED. Defendants rely on the same set of facts used to support its motion for summary judgment and fails to identify separate grounds for summary adjudication. (Code Civ. Proc. 437c(f)(1); CRC rules 3.1350(b) and (d).) Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jul-28-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES Via CourtCall:
Samuel Rosario of PHILLIPS SPALLAS & ANGSTADT LLP (415-278-9400), appearing for defendant Brisbane Hardware & Supply.

Not contested and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Brisbane Hardware & Supply's Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. Defendant failed to comply with the 75-day notice requirement under Code of Civil Procedure section 437c(a)(2). (see Code Civ. Proc. sections 1010.6(a)(4)(B), 12a(a), and 12c(a).) Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)
